english: one part; in a special sense: the sixth part of a rupee C.; k'a-c'ig (kha cig) part, some, several, frq. IV. (also Ssk. ख) resp. z'al (zhal), cf. k'a-po (kha po) 1. mouth, k'a k'a (kha kha) bitter mouth, bitter taste Med.; k'a dál-po (kha dal po) (soft month), manageable, tractable, k'a gyóṅ-po (kha gyong po) hard-mouthed, refractory; k'a sgyúr-ba (kha sgyur ba) (= k'a-lo sgy. (kha lo sgy.)) to govern, to rein the mouth (of a horse), to lead, guide, influence other persons Glr., to turn off (a river) Tar.; k'a 'tén-pa (kha 'then pa) (to pull the mouth) to stop a beast of draught Tar.; k'a byed-pa (kha byed pa), W, *p'é-c'e* (phe che) to open one's mouth, gdáṅs-pa (gdangs pa) to open it wide, 'dzúm-pa ('dzum pa), W., *cug-c'e* (cug che) to shut it; k'a brdáb-pa (kha brdab pa) (or kráb-pa (krab pa)?) to smack; *k'a dab* (kha dab) (or *tab* (tab)) *zér-wa* (zer wa) to produce a smacking, snapping sound, col.; k'a rég-pa (kha reg pa) c. dat. to put one's mouth to a thing, in order to eat or drink it; k'a 'júg-pa (kha 'jug pa) c. dat. to interfere, to meddle with; k'a tál-ba (kha tal ba) 1. col. = k'a 'jug-pa (kha 'jug pa), 2. Cs.: to promise; k'a gtúgs-pa (kha gtugs pa), k'a 'o gtúgs-pa (kha 'o gtugs pa), k'a-la 'o byéd-pa (kha la 'o byed pa), k'a sbyór-ba (kha sbyor ba) B., C., *k'a lán-c'e* (kha lan che) W, *k'a kyél-wa* (kha kyel wa) C. to kiss; *k'a kyé-c'e* (kha kye che) W., to inveigh, to give ill language; k'a bsré-ba (kha bsre ba) to have intercourse, social connexion with one another, viz. in eating, drinking and smoking together, which is a matter of no little social consequence; k'a 'dzin byéd-pa (kha 'dzin byed pa) c.genit. to receive friendly, to be kind to, assist Mil.; k'a gtád-pa (kha gtad pa) Glr. 16. 3. was explained: to bring together personally, to confront, = k'a sprád-pa (kha sprad pa); k'a búb-tu nyal-ba (kha bub tu nyal ba) to lie in that position; k'a bslán-ba (kha bslan ba) the contrary of the preceding; k'a 'óg-tu bltás-te s'i-ba (kha 'og tu bltas te shi ba) to be killed by a precipitous fall. Especially: the speaking mouth, k'a-nas (kha nas), col. also *k'á-na* (kha na), orally, by word of mouth, e. g. to state, report, *k'á-ne zér-na* (kha ne zer na) in the colloquial language C.; *k'a dé-mo nyiṅ sóg-po* (kha de mo nying sog po) W. hypocritical; k'a-la slá-te dón-la bká (kha la sla te don la bka') easily spoken after, but difficult to be understood (e. g. a doctrine); *k'a s'or soṅ* (kha shor song) 'my (his etc.) mouth has run away', *nor soṅ* (nor song) 'has erred', the former denoting inconsiderate talk, the latter a lapsus linguae; k'as lén-pa (khas len pa), bláṅ-ba (blang ba) 1. 'to anticipate with the mouth', to promise frq., with direct speech or term. inf., sometimes also with the term. of a sbst. e. g. brán-du k'as blaṅs (bran du khas blangs) he promised or engaged himself as a servant, -- also: to presume, to arrogate Mil. 2. 'to accept, adopt with the mouth', to acknowledge, admit Tar.; k'as c'é-ba (khas che ba) B., k'a tál-ba (kha tal ba) Cs., to promise; k'a sná-ba (kha sna ba), snás-pa (snas pa) to blurt out, speak out inconsiderately; k'a c'ám-pa (kha cham pa), mt'ún-pa (mthun pa), col. *t'úg-pa* (thug pa) to agree upon; k'a sdóm-pa (kha sdom pa), mnán-pa (mnan pa) to silence, W.; *k'a kág-c'e (kha kag che), kyil-c'e* (kyil che) id.; k'a skyór-ba (kha skyor ba), slú-ba (slu ba) to speak cunningly, to try to persuade etc.; k'a róg-pa (kha rog pa), more freq. *k'a róg-(te) dúg-pa (kha rog (te) dug pa), dád-pa (dad pa)*, to be silent; k'a p'áṅ-ba (kha phang ba) Tar., prob. = k'a 'k'yam dbyúṅ-pa (kha 'khyam dbyung pa) C. to divulge ill rumours; k'a lóg-pa (kha log pa) to reply, contradict; k'a gáṅ dgar smrá-ba (kha gang dgar smra ba) (*gaṅ t'ad (gang thad), gaṅ dran zér-c'e* (gang dran zer che) W.) to talk at random; k'a- (la) nyán-pa (kha (la) nyan pa) to obey, k'a nyán-po (kha nyan po) obedient (resp. bka' (bka') i.o. k'a (kha)); gsál-k'a (gsal kha) clear, intelligible language; k'a nán-du smrá-ba (kha nan du smra ba), W.: *k'a sóg-po zér-c'e* (kha sog po zer che) to use ill language; also without *nán-pa* (nan pa) or *sóg-po (sog po), k'a zér-c'e* (kha zer che) or *k'a tón-wa* (kha ton wa) means the same. -- 2. mouth, opening, orifice, of a vessel, cavern, pit etc., k'a gc'ód-pa (kha gchod pa), 'gébs-pa ('gebs pa) to cover, shut an opening; k'a byéd-pa (kha byed pa) to open, is also used of a book, a letter etc. (for holy books z'al (zhal) is employed i.o. k'a (kha)); k'a 'bye-ba (kha 'bye ba) to open or unclose itself, to begin to appear, k'a bú-ba (kha bu ba) id., of flowers; k'a búb-tu (kha bub tu) the opening turned downward, k'a bslán-du (kha bslan du) turned upward; k'a-t'úg skóṅ-ba (kha thug skong ba) to fill to the brim; k'a skóṅ-ba (kha skong ba) to fill up a void, to make up a deficiency, gz'an-nas (gzhan nas) or las (las) from elsewhere; k'a naṅ (kha nang) the inward brim, k'a p'yi (kha phyi) the outer edge Glr. -- 3. the front side, face, k'a lhor stón-pa (kha lhor ston pa) or ltá-ba (lta ba) to be directed southwards Glr. -- 4. surface, k'a 'brí-ba (kha 'bri ba), to be diminished, of a fluid the surface of which is sinking; k'a 'p'rí-ba (kha 'phri ba) to diminish, to make less, by taking away from the surface; the outside, k'a dkar gtíṅ nag (kha dkar gting nag) outside white, inside black, fig. Mil.; in a special sense: colour, v. k'á-dóg (kha dog); therefore k'á-ru (kha ru), k'á-na (kha na), k'á-la (kha la), k'ar (khar) 1. on, upon, above, s'iṅ-k'ar (shing khar) upon the tree (e. g. he sits), up
